title: Friedrich Pollock: The éminence grise of the Frankfurt School [Book Review]
author: Kellner

opening_move.description: The review opens by identifying Pollock as a neglected figure in Frankfurt School historiography — the first book-length study of the Pollock–Horkheimer relationship fills a gap in intellectual history.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Recovering Pollock's foundational role in shaping the Marxist and critical theory orientation of the Frankfurt School

rivals_engaged: previous Frankfurt School histories that marginalised Pollock
theoretical_positioning.description: The review is broadly sympathetic to Lenhard's project, offering summary and contextualisation without a strong independent normative stance.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: intellectual history / genealogical reconstruction of Frankfurt School development
epistemic_mode.notes: Book review of an intellectual biography; the argument is primarily expository and contextualising rather than normatively interventionist.

case_type.case_name: Frankfurt School Institute for Social Research; Friedrich Pollock's career

confidence_notes: Book review genre; largely expository. Low normative stake reflects reviewer's descriptive-appreciative stance rather than argumentative intervention.
